item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan on alert as Chinese warships approach Penghu Islands
item.description: Taiwan detected a Chinese destroyer and frigate operating near the Penghu islands, home to major Taiwanese military bases, prompting Taipei to deploy naval and air forces to monitor the vessels.

item.title: 🇩🇪 German Chancellor links Ukraine peace to territorial concessions
item.description: Friedrich Merz suggested Ukraine may need to cede territory to Russia as part of a peace deal, framing such concessions as a potential pathway to EU membership for Kyiv.

item.title: 🇰🇵 North Korea opens memorial for troops killed in Ukraine war
item.description: Kim Jong Un inaugurated a memorial museum honoring North Korean soldiers killed fighting alongside Russian forces in Ukraine, pledging continued support for Moscow's war effort during a ceremony attended by Russia's defense minister.

item.title: ⛪ Pope Leo meets first female Archbishop of Canterbury in historic encounter
item.description: Pope Leo XIV prayed with Archbishop of Canterbury Sarah Mullally at the Vatican, vowing to overcome differences in a historic meeting with the first female leader of the Church of England and the global Anglican Communion.

item.title: 🇷🇺🇮🇷 Putin Pledges Support for Iran in St. Petersburg Talks with Foreign Minister Araghchi
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin met with Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi in St. Petersburg, offering support to navigate the "difficult period" while expressing hope for peace. Moscow has resisted being drawn into the Middle East conflict while continuing its invasion of Ukraine.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Lebanese President Aoun Rejects "Wars Serving Foreign Interests" in Rebuke to Hezbollah
item.description: President Joseph Aoun pushed back against critics of negotiations with Israel, declaring that "traitors are those who drag Lebanon into a war to serve foreign interests" — a direct criticism of Hezbollah's wars "in support of Gaza and Iran" rather than Lebanon's interests.

item.title: 🇦🇺⛽ Australia Coalition Proposes $800M Plan to Double Fuel Reserves
item.description: Australia's federal Coalition unveiled an $800 million plan to double the nation's minimum fuel reserves to 60 days, citing energy security concerns amid the Middle East conflict. The proposal adds at least one billion litres of new onshore storage with a focus on diesel.

item.title: 🇮🇷 US reviews latest Iranian proposal to end war stalemate
item.description: President Trump discussed a new Iranian proposal with his national security team as Tehran's foreign minister headed to Russia. The proposal reportedly offers to reopen the Strait of Hormuz in exchange for ending the US blockade.

item.title: 🇧🇭 Bahrain strips 69 people of citizenship over Iran support
item.description: Bahrain revoked citizenship of 69 people for what it called sympathy with Iran's hostile acts and collaboration with foreign entities. Rights groups condemned the move as a 'blatant abuse of power'.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US Supreme Court reinstates Texas electoral map favoring Republicans
item.description: The conservative-majority Supreme Court reinstated a redrawn Texas electoral map that could flip up to five Democratic-held House seats to Republicans. The ruling overturned a lower court decision that had found the map racially discriminatory.

item.title: 💰 Dollar eases as US-Iran talks stall ahead of Powell's final Fed meeting
item.description: The US dollar weakened against the euro as diplomatic impasse in US-Iran negotiations weighed on markets. Investors are also watching for Jerome Powell's expected final Federal Reserve meeting this week.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Suspect charged with attempted assassination of Trump after correspondents dinner shooting
item.description: Cole Tomas Allen, 31, was charged with attempted assassination after allegedly trying to storm the White House Correspondents' Association dinner with weapons. He made his first federal court appearance.

item.title: 🇬🇧 King Charles arrives in US for state visit amid Iran war tensions
item.description: Britain's King Charles and Queen Camilla began a four-day state visit to the US, meeting President Trump at the White House. The trip comes as the UK and US differ over the Iran war approach.

item.title: ⛪ Pope Leo meets first female Archbishop of Canterbury in historic Vatican encounter
item.description: Pope Leo and Archbishop Sarah Mullally met for the first time at the Vatican, exchanging gifts and praying together in a symbolic step toward unity between the Catholic Church and Church of England.

item.title: 🇮🇱 IDF Chief Condemns Unethical Soldier Conduct as Discipline Erodes
item.description: Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir warned that discipline has eroded in recent wars, citing incidents including soldiers smashing a Jesus statue in Lebanon and wearing unapproved patches.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Blocks Meta From Acquiring AI Startup Manus
item.description: Beijing tightened scrutiny of AI industry amid intensifying geopolitical rivalry with the US over technology, moving to block Meta from acquiring the AI startup.

item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan Completes Major US Tank Deal With Final Abrams Shipment
item.description: The acquisition marks the biggest upgrade to Taiwan armored forces in decades as it bolsters northern defense capabilities against Chinese threats.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran ceasefire negotiations stall as both sides dig in on demands
item.description: Diplomatic efforts to end the Iran war have stalled over the weekend, with both Iran and the US maintaining hardline positions. The stalemate comes two months into the conflict that has disrupted global oil supplies and heightened regional tensions.

item.title: 💰 Iran war economic strain spreads across emerging markets
item.description: Two months into the Iran war, emerging and developing economies are feeling the economic toll through rising inflation, fiscal strains, and trade disruptions. The conflict has extended its reach beyond the Middle East, affecting markets across Asia, Africa, and Latin America.

item.title: 🛢️ Iran war may accelerate international talks to exit fossil fuels
item.description: The Iran conflict has disrupted oil and gas supplies and sent prices spiking, potentially invigorating discussions at IMO and Santa Marta meetings on accelerating the energy transition. Countries worldwide are reconsidering their approach to energy security.

item.title: 🌏 Asia Could Emerge as Unexpected Winner from Iran War Fallout
item.description: Despite being heavily reliant on Middle East energy, Asia may benefit long-term from accelerated cybersecurity investment, fossil fuel diversification, and supply chain restructuring prompted by the conflict.

item.title: 🇰🇵🇷🇺 North Korea and Russia Deepen Alliance at Memorial for Fallen Soldiers in Ukraine
item.description: At a ceremony honoring North Korean soldiers killed fighting in Ukraine, Moscow offered to renew a military pact with Pyongyang, strengthening the anti-West coalition as the war drags on.

item.title: 🕵️⚔️ Ukraine Disrupts Russian-Linked Network Planning Assassinations and Sabotage Across EU
item.description: Prosecutors announced an international operation disrupted a network preparing killings, arson, and infrastructure attacks across multiple European countries, linked to Russian intelligence.

item.title: 🚙🔋 US Army Developing Heavier Hybrid-Powered Infantry Squad Vehicle
item.description: The Army is testing a new hybrid-powered ISV-H vehicle designed to carry more armor and equipment while maintaining mobility for infantry squads in rugged terrain.

item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan president cancels Africa visit after China pressures three nations to deny flight permits
item.description: President Lai Ching-te postponed his visit to Eswatini after Seychelles, Mauritius and Madagascar withdrew flight permissions under Chinese pressure, in what Taiwan calls blatant interference in other countries affairs.
